Description.Text: Historical AWS Cost & Usage Report (CUR) data aggregated WITHOUT resource_id (provides an approximate resource_id_count instead). Use for cost/usage trends, billing analysis, service/account/region breakdowns, and Reserved Instance / Savings Plan coverage & savings. Default window is 7 months: the most recent 3 full months at DAILY granularity (usage_date), older months rolled up to MONTHLY. For per-resource detail use resource_view; for hourly detail use hourly_view.

InlineCustomInstruction.InstructionText:
FIELD NAMES
All field names in these instructions are the dataset (QuickSight/SPICE) field names - reference them exactly as written. In the CUR COLUMN MAPPING below, the format is `dataset_field -> source CUR column`: the right side is the original AWS Cost and Usage Report column, shown so you can apply standard CUR knowledge. Query using the dataset_field (left side), not the CUR column.

WHEN TO USE
Use summary_view for historical trend analysis, billing summaries, cost breakdowns by service/account/region, RI/SP coverage & savings, and discount/credit analysis. Do NOT use for per-resource questions (no resource_id) or sub-daily analysis. If asked about a specific resource_id, use resource_view. For hourly patterns, use hourly_view.

GRANULARITY & WINDOW
- ~7 months of data. usage_date is DAILY for the most recent 3 full calendar months and MONTHLY for older periods - account for this when computing daily averages over the full range.
- billing_period is the first day of the billing month. year/month are derived from billing_period for easy grouping.

COST COLUMNS (use amortized_cost as the default cost metric)
- amortized_cost: effective cost with RI/SP upfront & recurring fees spread across the term. Best representation of true cost.
- unblended_cost: raw invoice cost at line-item level. Use for matching the actual bill.
- public_cost: on-demand/list-price equivalent. Compare vs amortized_cost to quantify RI/SP + discount savings.
- ri_sp_trueup, ri_sp_upfront_fees: RI/SP amortization components.

CATEGORIZATION
- charge_type: raw CUR line_item_line_item_type (Usage, DiscountedUsage, SavingsPlanCoveredUsage, RIFee, Fee, Tax, Credit, Refund, SavingsPlanRecurringFee, etc.).
- charge_category: 'Running_Usage' (Usage, DiscountedUsage, SavingsPlanCoveredUsage) vs 'non_usage' (everything else: credits, refunds, fees, tax, discounts).
- purchase_option: OnDemand | Reserved | SavingsPlan | Spot.

CUR COLUMN MAPPING (columns map ~1:1 to CUR; use standard CUR knowledge)
billing_period -> bill_billing_period_start_date
usage_date -> line_item_usage_start_date (see granularity note)
payer_account_id -> bill_payer_account_id
linked_account_id -> line_item_usage_account_id
invoice_id -> bill_invoice_id
charge_type -> line_item_line_item_type
product_code -> line_item_product_code
service -> product_servicecode (friendly name; falls back to line_item_product_code)
product_name -> product_product_name
operation -> line_item_operation
usage_type -> line_item_usage_type
item_description -> line_item_line_item_description
pricing_unit -> pricing_unit
region -> product_region
availability_zone -> line_item_availability_zone
instance_type -> product_instance_type
instance_type_family -> product_instance_type_family
platform -> product_operating_system
database_engine -> product_database_engine
tenancy -> product_tenancy
processor -> product_physical_processor
processor_features -> product_processor_features
current_generation -> product_current_generation
product_family -> product_product_family
product_group -> product_group
product_from_location / product_to_location -> product_from_location, product_to_location
legal_entity -> line_item_legal_entity
billing_entity -> bill_billing_entity

Cost sources: unblended_cost -> line_item_unblended_cost; public_cost -> pricing_public_on_demand_cost; usage_quantity -> line_item_usage_amount; amortized_cost is derived from savings_plan_* and reservation_* effective/fee columns. ri_sp_arn -> savings_plan_savings_plan_a_r_n or reservation_reservation_a_r_n.

RI/SP METADATA
ri_sp_arn -> ARN of the RI or Savings Plan (empty for on-demand/spot)
ri_sp_offering, ri_sp_payment (All/Partial/No Upfront), ri_sp_term (1yr/3yr), ri_sp_end_date -> RI/SP commitment attributes (populated via join; may be empty).

USAGE & RESOURCES
- usage_quantity: consumed quantity in pricing_unit (usage-type line items only).
- resource_id_count: APPROXIMATE distinct-resource count per row (approx_distinct). Indicates resource density; not an exact count and cannot identify individual resources.

ACCOUNT MAPPING
account_id / account_name: friendly account identifiers (may be NULL if mapping not configured). *_mapping columns are lookup/join keys - not for display.

ORGANIZATIONAL TAXONOMY (may be present)
Depending on the CUR and this deployment's configuration, additional columns for organizational taxonomy may be present - user-defined cost allocation tags (typically prefixed resource_tags_ / user_) and AWS Cost Categories (typically prefixed cost_category_). When present, use them to group cost by organizational dimensions such as team, environment, cost center, project, or business unit (chargeback/showback). Inspect available columns at query time; do not assume a fixed set.

QUERY TIPS
- Monthly trend: GROUP BY billing_period (or year, month).
- Daily trend: GROUP BY usage_date (only meaningful within the last 3 months).
- By service/account: GROUP BY service / linked_account_id (or account_name if populated).
- Savings: compare SUM(public_cost) vs SUM(amortized_cost), or filter purchase_option.
- Discounts/credits: filter charge_category = 'non_usage' or specific charge_type values.
